quotes.text: The further backward you look, the further forward you can see.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: History teaches us that men behave wisely once they have exhausted all other alternatives.
quotes.author: Abba Eban

quotes.text: The only thing new in the world is the history you do not know.
quotes.author: Harry S. Truman

quotes.text: The march of freedom and democracy will leave Marxism on the ash heap of history.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: A generation which ignores history has no past and no future.
quotes.author: Robert A. Heinlein

quotes.text: Ideas shape the course of history more than any other force.
quotes.author: John Maynard Keynes

quotes.text: The very ink with which history is written is merely fluid prejudice.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: What experience and history teach us is that people have never learned anything from history.
quotes.author: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel

quotes.text: The duty of youth is to challenge corruption.
quotes.author: Kurt Cobain

quotes.text: Patriotism is not short frenzied outbursts of emotion, but the tranquil and steady dedication of a lifetime.
quotes.author: Adlai Stevenson

quotes.text: Every gun that is made, every warship launched, signifies a theft from those who hunger and are not fed.
quotes.author: Dwight D. Eisenhower

quotes.text: The reasonable man adapts himself to the world; the unreasonable one persists in trying to adapt the world to himself; therefore all progress depends on the unreasonable man.
quotes.author: George Bernard Shaw

quotes.text: Life is an exciting business, and most exciting when it is lived for others, for in giving of ourselves we discover the truest measure of our worth.
quotes.author: Helen Keller

quotes.text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are, and to do so requires the courage to shed everything that is not authentically you.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate, never knowing it was your own mind all along.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: The meeting of two personalities is like the contact of two chemical substances; if there is any reaction, both are transformed forever.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Everything that irritates us about others can lead us to an understanding of ourselves, for we see in them what we refuse to see in ourselves.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Your vision will become clear only when you can look into your own heart; who looks outside dreams; who looks inside awakens to the truth.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: The shoe that fits one person pinches another; there is no recipe for living that suits all cases, for each must find their own way.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: There is a great deal of unmapped country within us which would have to be taken into account in an explanation of our gusts and storms.
quotes.author: George Eliot

quotes.text: It is never too late to be what you might have been, for within each of us lies the seed of every possibility we have ever dreamed of.
quotes.author: George Eliot

quotes.text: What do we live for, if it is not to make life less difficult for each other, to share the burden and lighten the load of our fellow travelers.
quotes.author: George Eliot

quotes.text: The strongest principle of growth lies in human choice, and the power to choose wisely is the most valuable gift we can cultivate.
quotes.author: George Eliot

quotes.text: Adventure is worthwhile in itself, and the call of the horizon is something that lives deep in the heart of every man and woman.
quotes.author: Amelia Earhart

quotes.text: The most effective way to do it is to do it, without hesitation and without waiting for the perfect moment that may never arrive.
quotes.author: Amelia Earhart

quotes.text: The most difficult thing is the decision to act; the rest is merely tenacity; the fears are paper tigers that vanish when confronted.
quotes.author: Amelia Earhart

quotes.text: There are years that ask questions and years that answer, and the wisdom of life comes from knowing which season you are in.
quotes.author: Zora Neale Hurston

quotes.text: Ships at a distance have every man's wish on board, and for some they come in with the tide; for others they sail forever on the horizon.
quotes.author: Zora Neale Hurston

quotes.text: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes that see what has always been there.
quotes.author: Marcel Proust

quotes.text: We do not receive wisdom; we must discover it for ourselves after a journey that no one can take for us or spare us from.
quotes.author: Marcel Proust

quotes.text: Let us read, and let us dance; these two amusements will never do any harm to the world, and they bring joy to all who partake.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: Judge a man by his questions rather than by his answers, for the quality of the question reveals the depth of the mind.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: Every man is guilty of all the good he did not do, and the weight of those missed opportunities is heavier than any sin.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: Common sense is not so common, and the rarest of all human qualities is the ability to see clearly what lies before our eyes.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: I disapprove of what you say, but I will defend to the death your right to say it, for freedom of thought is the foundation of all liberty.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: The art of medicine consists of amusing the patient while nature cures the disease, for the body has its own remarkable wisdom.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: The earth does not belong to man; man belongs to the earth, and all things are connected like the blood that unites one family.
quotes.author: Chief Seattle

quotes.text: When the last tree is cut down, the last fish eaten, and the last stream poisoned, you will realize that you cannot eat money.
quotes.author: Native American Proverb

quotes.text: If your actions inspire others to dream more, learn more, do more and become more, you are a leader worthy of the title.
quotes.author: John Quincy Adams

quotes.text: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge is the only guardian of true liberty, for an educated people cannot be enslaved.
quotes.author: James Madison

quotes.text: We are not combatants here; we are citizens, and our duty is to build, to cultivate, and to preserve the blessings of our nation.
quotes.author: Dwight D. Eisenhower

quotes.text: The hottest places in hell are reserved for those who, in times of great moral crisis, maintain their neutrality and refuse to choose.
quotes.author: Dante Alighieri

quotes.text: There is no greater sorrow than to recall happiness in times of misery, and yet the memory itself is a kind of treasure.
quotes.author: Dante Alighieri
